description_long: This is a traditional multi-tiered pagoda structure, featuring three prominent stories separated by wide, overhanging blue roofs with distinct upward-curving eaves. The building is supported by a central vertical core, with each level surrounded by red railings that add both structural and decorative elements. The roofs are adorned with red accents at the corners, and the topmost section is crowned with an ornate, orange finial. The base of the pagoda is a raised platform with a staircase leading up to the main entrance, suggesting accessibility for visitors. The walls of the structure are a muted gray, possibly stone or concrete, with subtle weathering visible. The overall design is emblematic of East Asian architecture, particularly Chinese pagodas, and would serve as a cultural or religious landmark in an urban park or garden.
description: A three-story traditional pagoda with blue, upward-curving roofs, red railings, and an ornate orange finial, set on a raised platform with a staircase. The structure features classic East Asian architectural elements and serves as a striking cultural landmark.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main staircase, central entrance, and symmetrical arrangement of blue roofs and red railings.
description_view_1: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, but with less emphasis on the entrance and staircase details.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the depth of the overhanging roofs and the layered structure of the pagoda.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 2, showing the stacked roof tiers and red railings from a different angle.
